passages.title: Bondi Beach
passages.source_html:
Bondi Beach 


A


Bondi Beach, Australia’s most famous beach, is located in the suburb of Bondi, in the Local Government Area of Waverley, seven kilometers from the centre of Sydney. ‘Bondi’ or ‘Boondi’ is an Aboriginal word meaning water breaking over rocks or the sound of breaking waves. The Australian Museum records that Bondi means a place where a flight of nullas took place. There are Aboriginal Rock carving on the northern end of the beach at Ben Buckler and south of Bondi Beach near McKenzies Beach on the coastal walk. 


B 


The indigenous people of the area at the time of European settlement have generally been welcomed to as the Sydney people or the Eora (Eora means ‘the people’). One theory describes the Eora as a sub-group of the Darug language group which occupied the Cumberland Plain west to the Blue Mountains. However, another theory suggests that they were a distinct language group of their own. There is no clear evidence for the name or names of the particular band(s) of the Eora that roamed what is now the Waverley area. A number of place names within Waverley, most famously Bondi, have been based on words derived from Aboriginal languages of the Sydney region. 


C 


From the mid-1800s Bondi Beach was a favourite location for family outings and picnics. The beginnings of the suburb go back to 1809, when the early road builder, William Roberts, received from Governor Bligh a grant of 81 hectares of what is now most of the business and residential area of Bondi Beach. In 1851, Edward Smith Hall and Francis O’Brien purchased 200 acres of the Bondi area that embraced almost the whole frontage of Bondi Beach, and it was named the ‘The Bondi Estate.’ Between 1855 and 1877 O’Brien purchased Hall’s share of the land, renamed the land the ‘O’Brien Estate,’ and made the beach and the surrounding land available to the public as a picnic ground and amusement resort. As the beach became increasingly popular, O’Brien threatened to stop public beach access. However, the Municipal Council believed that the Government needed to intervene to make the beach a public reserve. 


D 


During the 1900s beach became associated with health, leisure and democracy – a playground everyone could enjoy equally. Bondi Beach was a working-class suburb throughout most of the twentieth century with migrant people from New Zealand comprising the majority of the local population. The first tramway reached the beach in 1884. Following this, tram became the first public transportation in Bondi. As an alternative, this action changed the rule that only rich people can enjoy the beach. By the 1930s Bondi was drawing not only local visitors but also people from elsewhere in Australia and overseas. Advertising at the time referred to Bondi Beach as the ‘Playground of the Pacific’. 


E 


There is a growing trend that people prefer having to relax near seaside instead of living unhealthily in cities. The increasing popularity of sea bathing during the late 1800s and early 1900s raised concerns about public safety and how to prevent people from drowning. In response, the world’s first formally documented surf lifesaving club, the Bondi Surf Bathers’ Life Saving Club, was formed in 1907. This was powerfully reinforced by the dramatic events of ‘Black Sunday’ at Bondi in 1938. Some 35,000 people were on the beach and a large group of lifesavers were about to start a surf race when three freak waves hit the beach, sweeping hundreds of people out to sea. Lifesavers rescued 300 people. The largest mass rescue in the history of surf bathing, it confirmed the place of the lifesaver in the national imagination. 


F 


Bondi Beach is the endpoint of the City to Surf Fun Run which is held each year in August. Australian surf carnivals further instilled this image. A Royal Surf Carnival was held at Bondi Beach for Queen Elizabeth II during her first visit in Australia in 1954. Since 1867, there have been over fifty visits by a member of the British Royal Family to Australia. In addition to many activities, the Bondi Beach Markets is open every Sunday. Many wealthy people spend Christmas Day at the beach. However, the shortage of houses occurs when lots of people crushed to the seaside. Manly is the seashore town which solved this problem. However, people still choose Bondi as the satisfied destination rather than Manly. 


G 


Bondi Beach has a commercial area along Campbell Parade and adjacent side streets, featuring many popular cafes, restaurants, and hotels, with views of the contemporary beach. It is depicted as wholly modern and European. In the last decade, Bondi Beaches’ unique position has seen a dramatic rise in svelte houses and apartments to take advantage of the views and scent of the sea. The valley running down to the beach is the famous world over for its view of distinctive red-tiled roofs. Those architectures are deeply influenced by British coastal town. 


H 

Bondi Beach hosted the beach volleyball competition at the 2000 Summer Olympics. A temporary 10,000-seat stadium, a much smaller stadium, 2 warm-up courts, and 3 training courts were set up to host the tournament. The Bondi Beach Volleyball Stadium was constructed for it and stood for just six weeks. Campaigners oppose both the social and environmental consequences of the development. The stadium will divide the beach in two and seriously restrict public access for swimming, walking, and other forms of outdoor recreation. People protest for their human rights of having a pure seaside and argue for health life in Bondi. 


I 


‘They’re prepared to risk lives and risk the Bondi beach environment for the sake of eight days of volleyball,’ said Stephen Uniacke, a construction lawyer involved in the campaign. Other environmental concerns include the possibility that soil dredged up from below the sand will acidify when brought to the surface.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-5


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? In boxes 1-5 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


1

The name of the Bondi beach is first called by the British settlers.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：A number of place names within Waverley, most famously Bondi, have been based on words derived from Aboriginal languages of the Sydney region.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是Bondi海滩的名字是否是由英国定居者首次命名的。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"the name of the Bondi beach"和"first called by the British settlers"定位到B段，特别是最后一句"A number of place names within Waverley, most famously Bondi, have been based on words derived from Aboriginal languages of the Sydney region."。

解题思路：根据原文B段最后一句可知，Waverley地区内许多地名，其中最著名的是Bondi，这些地名是基于源自悉尼地区土著语言的词汇。这说明Bondi的名字来源于土著语言，而不是由英国定居者首次命名的。因此，题目中的说法与原文信息矛盾，答案为False。


2

The aboriginal culture in Australia is different when compared with European culture.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：There is no clear evidence for the name or names of the particular band(s) of the Eora that roamed what is now the Waverley area. A number of place names within Waverley, most famously Bondi, have been based on words derived from Aboriginal languages of the Sydney region.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是“与欧洲文化相比，澳大利亚的土著文化是不同的”这一陈述是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“Aboriginal culture”和“European culture”来定位答案。在B段中，提到了“Aboriginal languages”和“place names”，但没有直接比较土著文化和欧洲文化的差异。

解题思路：题目陈述的是土著文化与欧洲文化的比较，而文章中并未提及两者之间的比较，只是提到了土著语言和地名。因此，文章没有提供足够的信息来支持或反驳这一陈述，所以答案是“NOT GIVEN”。


3

Bondi beach area holds many contemporary hotels.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Bondi Beach has a commercial area along Campbell Parade and adjacent side streets, featuring many popular cafes, restaurants, and hotels, with views of the contemporary beach. It is depicted as wholly modern and European.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“邦迪海滩地区拥有许多现代酒店”是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"contemporary hotels"（现代酒店）定位到G段。G段提到Bondi Beach的Campbell Parade及其周边街道有许多流行的咖啡馆、餐厅和酒店，这些地方都是现代风和欧洲风格。

解题思路：原文提到Bondi Beach的商业区有咖啡馆、餐厅和酒店，可饱览现代海滩风光，这些地方都是现代风和欧洲风格，但并未明确说明这些酒店是“contemporary”（现代的）。因此，题目中的信息在文章中没有明确对应，无法确定是否正确。所以答案为Not Given。


4

The seaside town in Bondi is affected by British culture for its characteristic red color.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：The valley running down to the beach is the famous world over for its view of distinctive red-tiled roofs. Those architectures are deeply influenced by British coastal town.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“邦迪海滨的沿海城镇受到英国文化的影响，以其独特的红色屋顶而闻名”是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"seaside town in Bondi"、"characteristic red color"和"British culture"定位到G段最后一句，提到邦迪海滩的建筑深受英国沿海城镇的影响，并且以其独特的红色屋顶而闻名。

解题思路：根据原文G段最后一句的描述，邦迪海滩的建筑确实受到英国沿海城镇的影响，并且以其独特的红色屋顶而闻名，这与题目所述一致。因此，答案为True。


5

Living near Bondi seashore is not beneficial for health.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：There is a growing trend that people prefer having to relax near seaside instead of living unhealthily in cities.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问“在邦迪海滨附近生活对健康没有好处”，需要判断这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“near Bondi seashore”和“not beneficial for health”定位到E段第一句，该句提到人们越来越倾向于在海边放松，而不是在不健康的城市里生活。

解题思路：原文明确指出人们选择在海边放松是为了避免不健康的生活，说明在海边生活是有益于健康的。题目说“在邦迪海滨附近生活对健康没有好处”与原文信息直接矛盾，因此答案为False。


Questions 6-9


Answer the questions below using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR NUMBERS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 6-9 on your answer sheet.


6

6 At the end of the 19th century, which public transport did people use to go to Bondi?

正确答案： 6 tram##Tram
Question 6

原文答案句：The first tramway reached the beach in 1884. Following this, tram became the first public transportation in Bondi.

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是在19世纪末，人们去Bondi时使用的公共交通工具是什么。  

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"end of the 19th century"和"public transport"。通过时间点"end of the 19th century"可以定位到D段，该段提到了，1884年，首条有轨电车线路延伸至海滩。  

解题思路：D段提到"the first tramway reached the beach in 1884"，1884年，首条有轨电车线路延伸至海滩。自此，电车成为邦迪地区首个公共交通工具。说明在19世纪末，人们开始使用电车作为公共交通工具去Bondi海滩。因此，答案是"tram"。


7

7 When did the British Royalty first visit Bondi?

正确答案： 7 1954
Question 7

原文答案句：A Royal Surf Carnival was held at Bondi Beach for Queen Elizabeth II during her first visited in Australia in 1954.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是英国皇室首次访问邦迪海滩的时间。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"British Royalty"和"first visit"可以定位到F段中提到的"Queen Elizabeth II"和"first visited in Australia in 1954"。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子"Queen Elizabeth II during her first visited in Australia in 1954"，可以确定英国皇室首次访问邦迪海滩的时间是1954年。


8

8 Which Olympic event did Bondi hold in the 2000 Sydney Olympic games?

正确答案： 8 beach volleyball##Beach Volleyball
Question 8

原文答案句：Bondi Beach hosted the beach volleyball competition at the 2000 Summer Olympics.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是2000年悉尼奥运会上Bondi海滩举办了哪个奥运项目。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"2000 Sydney Olympic games"和"Olympic event"可以定位到H段第一句，该句明确提到了Bondi Beach在2000年夏季奥运会上举办了某个比赛项目。

解题思路：根据定位到的句子，可以直接提取出"beach volleyball"作为答案。句子中明确指出Bondi Beach举办了"beach volleyball competition"（沙滩排球比赛），这与题目要求的奥运项目完全一致，因此答案为"beach volleyball"。


9

9 What would be damaged if the stadium was built for that Olympic event?

正确答案： 9 environment##Environment
Question 9

原文答案句：Campaigners oppose both the social and environmental consequences of the development.  

对应段落：H段  

审题：题目问的是如果为奥运会赛事建造体育场，会损害什么。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"stadium"和"Olympic event"定位到H段，找到关于建造体育场的内容。  

解题思路：原文提到"the development"（开发建设）会带来"social and environmental consequences"（不良的社会和环境影响），而题目问的是"damaged"（损害）的内容。结合上下文，这里的"development"指的就是建造体育场，因此建造体育场会损害"environment"（环境）。


Questions 10-13


Complete the summary below. Using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 10-13 on your answer sheet.


10

Bondi beach holds the feature sports activities every year, which attracts lot of 10 .

正确答案： 10 wealthy people##Wealthy People
Question 10

原文答案句：Many wealthy people spend Christmas Day at the beach.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问Bondi海滩每年举办特色体育活动吸引了很多什么。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"feature sports activities every year"（每年特色体育活动）可以定位到F段提到的"City to Surf Fun Run"（城市到海滩跑步活动），该活动每年8月举行，属于特色体育活动。题目问这类活动吸引的人群，因此需要找到描述参与者或观众的句子。

解题思路：在F段中，提到特色体育活动后，后面提到"Many wealthy people spend Christmas Day at the beach."（许多富裕人士在圣诞节当天去海滩），虽然这句话直接提到的是圣诞节，但结合上下文，F段主要描述Bondi海滩的各种活动和人群，包括体育赛事和富裕人士的参与。因此可以推断，特色体育活动吸引的主要人群是"wealthy people"（富裕人士）。


11

Choosing to live at this place during the holidays. But local accommodation cannot meet with the expanding population, a nearby town of 11 is the first suburb site to support the solution,

正确答案： 11 Manly##manly
Question 11

原文答案句：However, the shortage of houses occurs when lots of people crushed to the seaside. Manly is the seashore town which solved this problem. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是当地住宿无法满足日益增长的人口时，哪个附近的城镇是第一个提供解决方案的郊区地点。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"local accommodation cannot meet with the expanding population"和"nearby town"来定位答案。在F段，其中提到Manly是一个解决住宿问题的地点。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子，许多人来到海边度假，便出现了房屋短缺的问题。Manly这个海滨小镇解决了这一问题。因此，Manly是第一个提供解决方案的附近城镇。


12

yet people prefer 12 as their best choice.

正确答案： 12 Bondi##bondi
Question 12

原文答案句：However, people still choose Bondi as the satisfied destination rather than Manly.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问人们仍然将什么作为最佳选择。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"prefer"和"best choice"定位到F段中"choose Bondi as the satisfied destination"。

解题思路：原文明确提到人们仍然选择Bondi作为满意的目的地，与题干中的"best choice"对应，因此答案为Bondi。


13

Its seaside buildings are well-known in the world for the special scenic colored 13 on buildings and the joyful smell from the sea.

正确答案： 13 tiled roofs##Tiled Roofs
Question 13

原文答案句：The valley running down to the beach is the famous world over for its view of distinctive red-tiled roofs.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目在问那些海滨建筑闻名于世，得益于其建筑有色彩的什么，以及海面飘来的沁人气息。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"seaside buildings"和"special scenic colored"定位到G段中描述建筑特色的句子。

解题思路：原文中明确提到"red-tiled roofs"是世界闻名的特色，题干中的"special scenic colored"对应原文的"distinctive red"，因此答案为"tiled roofs"（瓦屋顶。因此答案为"tiled roofs"。
